LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT UNITED PARCEL SERVICE, INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE PUNITIVE DAMAGES CLAIM AND IRRELEVANT PORTIONS FROM THE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. THE FOLLOWING PORTIONS OF THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT ("FAC") ARE HEREBY STRICKEN: (1) THAT PORTION OF PARAGRAPH 116, LINES 12 TO 13 WHICH READS "THEREFORE, PINZARRONE SEEKS AN AWARD OF PUNITIVE DAMAGES;" (2) PARAGRAPHS 108 THROUGH 116, INCLUSIVE; AND (3) PARAGRAPH D OF THE PRAYER ON PAGE 18 AS TO DEFENDANT UNITED PARCEL SERVICE, INC. THE REQUEST FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES IS STRICKEN BECAUSE THE FAC DOES NOT PLEAD FACTS ESTABLISHING THAT DEFENDANT ACTED WITH MALICE, OPPRESSION, OR FRAUD. (SEE CIV. CODE SEC. 3294.) THE ALLEGATIONS IN PARAGRAPHS 108 THROUGH 116 ARE STRICKEN AS IRRELEVANT. THE MOTION IS GRANTED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND IN LIGHT OF THE FACT THAT PLAINTIFF WAS PREVIOUSLY GRANTED LEAVE TO AMEND TO STATE FACTS TO SUPPORT A CLAIM FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES. ALTHOUGH THE MOTION WAS NOT FILED WITHIN THE TIME PERMITTED BY CCP SEC. 435, CCP SEC. 436 PROVIDES THAT THE COURT MAY STRIKE A PLEADING "AT ANY TIME AT ITS DISCRETION." PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: SHERYL L. SAWYER, CSR # 5976 (302)

ORDER DENYING PLACE 2 PARK CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR ALTERNATIVELY, MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF ISSUES
MINI MINUTES FOR JAN-13-2016 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT PLACE 2 PARK CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, ALTERNATIVELY, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF ISSUES IS DENIED. WHETHER DEFENDANT PLACE 2 PARK'S SIGN WAS FACTUAL CAUSE OF PLAINTIFF'S INJURIES IS A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT. "LIKE BREACH OF DUTY, CAUSATION ALSO IS ORDINARILY A QUESTION OF FACT WHICH CANNOT BE RESOLVED BY SUMMARY JUDGMENT. [CITATIONS]." (NICHOLS V. KELLER (1993) 15 CAL.APP.4TH 1672, 1687.) JUST BECAUSE PLAINTIFF DOES NOT "HAVE ANY IDEA" WHETHER THE CRACKED CONCRETE OR DEFENDANT PLACE 2 PARK'S SIGN CAUSED HER FALL, THAT DOES NOT MEAN THAT DEFENDANT PLACE 2 PARK'S SIGN WAS NOT AN ACTUAL AND PROXIMATE CAUSE OF HER INJURIES. (DEPOSITION OF PLAINTIFF AT 174:24-175:1.) THIS IS BECAUSE PLAINTIFF ALSO TESTIFIED AT HER DEPOSITION THAT DEFENDANT PLACE 2 PARK'S SIGN INTERFERED WITH HER MOVEMENT ON THE SIDEWALK. (ID., AT 97:19-98:19.) PLAINTIFF FURTHER TESTIFIED THAT SHE TRIED TO AVOID THE SIGN WHEN SHE FELL. (ID., AT 173:2-23.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976. (302/EG)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Notice Of Motion And Place 2 Park Corporations Motion For Summary Judgment Or, Alternatively, Summary Adjudication Of Issues
Off Calendar Jan-13-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Attorney Trevor McCann telephone number 925-270-7058 for Plaintiff.
James Picker of Philip M. Anderson & Associates telephone number 625-225-6838 Defendant Place 2 Park.
Heather Sweeney of Gordon Creed telephone number 415-421-3100 appeared by telephone for Defendant PG&E.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT PLACE 2 PARK CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, Alternatively, Summary Adjudication Of Issues is denied. Whether Defendant Place 2 Park's sign was factual cause of Plaintiff's injuries is a triable issue of material fact. "Like breach of duty, causation also is ordinarily a question of fact which cannot be resolved by summary judgment. [Citations]." (Nichols v. Keller (1993) 15 Cal.App.4th 1672, 1687.) Just because Plaintiff does not "have any idea" whether the cracked concrete or Defendant Place 2 Park's sign caused her fall, that does not mean that Defendant Place 2 Park's sign was not an actual and proximate cause of her injuries. (Deposition of Plaintiff at 174:24-175:1.) This is because Plaintiff also testified at her deposition that Defendant Place 2 Park's sign interfered with her movement on the sidewalk. (Id., at 97:19-98:19.) Plaintiff further testified that she tried to avoid the sign when she fell. (Id., at 173:2-23.) Order signed in open Court.

Judge: Ernest Goldsmith. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/EG)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Aug-03-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Roy Woo, of Philip M. Andersen & Associates, Tel. 925.225.6838, for defendants Park 2 Place Corp., appearing via CourtCall;
No other appearances in the matter.

Ruling: The matter was not argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT UNITED PARCEL SERVICE, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted. "There is a triable issue of material fact if, and only if, the evidence would allow a reasonable trier of fact to find the underlying fact in favor of the party opposing the motion in accordance with the applicable standard of proof." [See Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 C4th 826, 850.] To prevail in negligence, Plaintiff must show that Defendants owed a legal duty, that Defendants breached that duty, and that the breach proximately caused their injuries. [See Wiener v. Southcoast Childcare Centers, Inc., (2004) 32 Cal. 4th 1138, 1145.] When the pleaded facts do not naturally give rise to an inference of causation, plaintiff must plead specific facts "explaining how the conduct caused or contributed to the injury." Bockrath v. Aldrich Chem. Co., Inc. (1999) 21 C4th 71, 78. Under Saelzler v. Advanced Group 400 (2001) 25 Cal.4th 763, the court provided guidelines when determining whether causation exists. (1) proof cannot be based on speculation or conjecture; (2) Plaintiff must demonstrate substantial link between Defendant's conduct and injury; (3) Plaintiff must show it is more likely than not that Defendant's alleged breach was cause of injury; (4) cause cannot be inferred from hindsight; (5) the burden of proof on causation cannot be shifted to the defendant. Here, Defendant has met its initial burden by showing the essential element of causation to Plaintiff's negligence claim has not been established. Plaintiff does not raise a triable issue of material fact in opposition. Plaintiff alleges that UPS caused damage to the sidewalk, and this damage caused Plaintiff's fall and injuries. However, Plaintiff has not plead with specific facts which trucks caused the damage, when the damage occurred, or any admissible evidence showing Plaintiff's injury was caused by damage to the sidewalk. Plaintiff presents no evidence of a substantial link between UPS and Plaintiff's injuries. Plaintiff has not shown it was more likely than not that UPS caused Plaintiff's injuries. Thus, because Plaintiff has not established a prima facie case of causation, Defendant is entitled to summary judgment. Prevailing party shall prepare form of order that complies with CRC 3.1312. Judge Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk Felicia Green; Court Reporter Melanie Gheno, CSR No. 7489; Dept. 302. (302/EHG)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-22-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT PLACE 2 PARK CORP.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT - Defendant Place 2 Park Corp.'s demurrers to causes of action 2 and 3 are sustained without leave to amend. Both causes of action rely on a statute/ordinance that only applies to owners of real property and plaintiff alleges that defendant is merely a lessee. (Amended Complaint, pars. 17-21). In addition, negligence per se is an evidentiary presumption, not a distinct claim. See Das v. Bank of America, N.A., (2010) 186 Cal.App.4th 727, 737-738. The court overrules the demurrer to "cause of action" 8 because the cited provision sought to protect pedestrians such as plaintiff. Order signed. Judge: Ernest H. Off Calendar Nov-06-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT UNITED PARCEL SERVICE, INC.'s MOTION TO STRIKE PUNITIVE DAMAGES CLAIM AND IRRELEVANT PORTIONS FROM THE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is Granted without leave to amend. The following portions of the First Amended Complaint ("FAC") are hereby stricken: (1) That portion of Paragraph 116, Lines 12 to 13 which reads "Therefore, Pinzarrone seeks an award of punitive damages;" (2) Paragraphs 108 through 116, inclusive; and (3) Paragraph D of the Prayer on page 18 as to Defendant United Parcel Service, Inc. The request for punitive damages is stricken because the FAC does not plead facts establishing that Defendant acted with malice, oppression, or fraud. (See Civ. Code sec. 3294.) The allegations in paragraphs 108 through 116 are stricken as irrelevant. The motion is granted without leave to amend in light of the fact that Plaintiff was previously granted leave to amend to state facts to support a claim for punitive damages. Although the motion was not filed within the time permitted by CCP sec. 435, CCP sec. 436 provides that the Court may strike a pleading "at any time at its discretion." Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =(302/EHG)
